programmes.show_description: The East Anglian Air Ambulance crew battle to save driver Gerry's partially amputated hand. Motorcyclist Jamie has sustained multiple injuries following a collision with a van.
Constance is on a mission to get the listing for a celebrity singer's £12.5m home, Guy values a quaint cottage in Henley-on-Thames, and Diana takes on a high-end rental.
Juniper Hill Inn (Part 1/2): Gordon visits the historic Juniper Hill Inn in Vermont, which looks like a million dollars but smells of sewage...

programmes.show_name: The Art of Racing in the Rain
programmes.show_description: (2019) Touching comedy-drama. An ageing golden retriever (voiced by Kevin Costner) tells the story of his life with his best pal, a budding Formula 1 driver.
There are home truths, awkward questions and a generous helping of bickering, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party in Essex.
In the West Midlands, Kirstie helps neurosurgeon Naomi escape from a crowded house, while Phil meets couple Mo and Jon, who want completely different things from a home.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mike Bubbins in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
The Boys in the Bar: Sam supports his former baseball teammate Tom when he announces that he's gay in his new autobiography and holds a press event for the book at the bar.
Diane's Perfect Date: Sam and Diane challenge each other to find their perfect dates, but Sam ends up grabbing a highly unsuitable partner for Diane from the pool room.
No Contest: Diane's furious when Sam enters her photo into the Miss Boston Barmaid contest, until she realises it'll give her the chance to denounce the event to the media.
Boys' Therapy: Debra and Marie pressure Ray and Frank to join Robert at one of his therapy sessions. Instead, they head to the racetrack.
Debra's Parents: Debra invites her divorced parents over for Thanksgiving. When Ray walks in on them making love, he and Debra mistakenly assume that they're getting back together.
A Job for Robert: Ray's jealous when Marie starts treating Robert better than him.
And the Dish Ran Away with the Spoon: In a double-length episode, Niles and Daphne discover that there's a price to pay for admitting their true love.
The Bad Son: Frasier begs Martin to take a tour of a senior citizens' home so that he can ask out its manager.
The Great Crane Robbery: Frasier's bootlicking routine backfires when he invites his new boss, a nouveau-riche internet millionaire, to his tastefully decorated home.
Mama Maria's: Gordon Ramsay visits Mama Maria's, a failing restaurant in Brooklyn, New York, where owner John is overwhelmed by running a business that's become stuck in the past.
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Lancashire, Simon O'Brien decides to turn a 1980s glass cabinet into a modern grandfather clock, while Henry Cole relives his youth with a drum kit.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: Matt gets to work on a set of stone steps for the paddock and helps beekeeper George transport some of the beehives to the high Dales for heather season.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mike Bubbins in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Sharon and Brendon want to buy a holiday home beside beautiful Lake Garda, but they each have very different priorities... Can Lucy Alexander fulfil their dreams?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Portugal, Richard and Simon's bar relaunches after closing for a week. In Italy, a drought causes problems at Lisa and Paul's holiday lets. Plus: mattress mayhem in France.
The new run continues. Mark and Anne host at their Margate townhouse, where the atmosphere turns icy over ice cream at the beach thanks to one guest who can't forget about his own feedback.
The new run continues. In Sheffield, Lindsey serves up some antioxidant-stuffed dishes. But her soup is likened to baby food and her main makes a guest retch at the table. (Ep4/5)
...the Sun: With Jasmine's husband Jon out of action after his accident, her dad comes to help decorate. And fellow presenter Laura Hamilton treats Jasmine to a trip into Málaga. (Ep4)
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
Laura and Daniel want their son to have the same idyllic childhood that Laura had on Mersea Island in Essex. But converting an old beach house on a tiny budget becomes a nightmare.
Epic rags-to-riches drama with Brenda Blethyn and Jessica Reynolds. As the war rages on, Emma battles to keep her empire afloat, but would she really get into bed with the Fairleys? (Ep8/8)

programmes.show_name: New: Click to Kill: The AI War...
programmes.show_description: ...Machine: With unique access in Ukraine and Israel and to the US army, this documentary charts the new-tech arms race, from AI to drones. Will war become less destructive or more brutal?
Adrian has injuries to his face and has been bitten on his arm breaking up a fight in the city centre. Andy's had a motorbike accident. And Connor's fallen 12 feet off a roof.
A security guard manages a violent incident with a patient. Jack, who's 33, is rushed into A&E after falling from a horse. And seven-year-old Lyla has a finger infection.
Juniper Hill Inn (Part 2/2): The issues keep coming at the Juniper Hill Inn in Vermont. Can Gordon help the staff and owners to communicate? Or will he be forced to abandon ship?
The epic adventure reality contest continues. Predator turns on Predator, but will it pay off? One player goes all in on their biggest threat, and a fiery clash sets rivalries alight. (Ep5)
The epic adventure reality contest continues. With five Predators and two Prey, the players face a brutal reset - and a shock decision delivers the biggest upset of the game so far. (Ep6)

programmes.show_name: Keir Starmer: Where Did It All...
programmes.show_description: ...Go Wrong? Dispatches: Journalist Lewis Goodall examines how the Starmer government became so unpopular so quickly, less than two years after the biggest election landslide in UK history.
In Cardiff and Newport, Rachel and Mark plan an Asian menu, Sue and Joyce serve 'posh' versions of classic dishes, including baked beans with chives, and there's a shock on the final night.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: This episode celebrates one of Kirstie's biggest builds as we look back at how Keeley and Terence completely overhauled their Woodford home beyond recognition.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mike Bubbins in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Pick a Con... Any Con: Sam sees Coach being taken for a ride by a so-called friend, so he and the gang plot to get the money back from the con artist in a friendly poker game.
Someone Single, Someone Blue: Diane is told she must marry within 24 hours or her mother will lose an inheritance. So she asks Sam to marry her on a strictly business basis.
Showdown (Part 1/2): Sam's jealous when his brother shows up at the bar and captivates everyone - including a smitten Diane - with his tap-dancing, magic tricks and singing.
A Date for Peter: Amy's parents ask Ray to give their son Peter some tips on dating. Debra throws a cocktail party, but Ray isn't impressed by the guest list.
Favors: Marie helps Debra to deal with Ray's bad habit of leaving his stuff lying around the house.
The Faux Pas: Ray accidentally insults the twins' new friend... to the boy's father. If that's not bad enough, he makes things worse by repeating his mistake.
Taking Liberties: Frasier hires a butler, who spoils everyone and offers Frasier sage advice in his bid to join the snobbish opera board.
Legal Tender Love and Care: Frasier starts dating the attractive attorney who defended him against Donny - who was jilted at the altar by Daphne.
(2010) Family fantasy comedy. Dwayne Johnson stars as a tough ice hockey player who is transformed into a real-life fairy as punishment for telling a child that tooth fairies don't exist.
(2006) Family action-thriller with Alex Pettyfer and Mickey Rourke. British intelligence recruit a schoolboy to take on the man who killed his dad and is planning to unleash a deadly virus.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mike Bubbins in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Jaazzmina and Mark want to create a remote Spanish smallholding and wellness retreat in Almería Province. Can Sara Damergi find the right spot for £100,000?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Portugal, there's beer fear at Richard and Simon's new bar. Will they run out? A group of cyclists tackle the French Pyrenees' snowy mountains.
The new run concludes. After a feisty week in Kent - when some B&Bers couldn't even wait until payment day to get their gripes off their chest - it is now actually payment day.
The new run continues. In Sheffield, Ambrose hopes that his tried-and-tested dishes can bag him the grand. After a barrage of questions and competitive chat, has his night won out? (Ep5/5)
...the Sun: Jon's still out of action but it's full steam ahead as Jasmine and her dad work to complete the kids' bedrooms and family bathroom. Will daughter Joy approve of her room? (Ep5)
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: Kirstie and Phil look back at Graham and Nardeth's worn-out Windsor home with a lacklustre layout. Did Kirstie's plan prevail, or did the list-it line-up win?
...Secrets Unwrapped: A fascinating look inside the Willy Wonka world of Cadbury across a make-or-break year, as they try to reduce the sugar in Dairy Milk and launch a Creme Egg campaign.
The Goggleboxers return for more of the biggest and best TV. What do they think this time?
Josh Pugh, Lorraine Kelly and Steve Bracknall join Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker for a tongue-in-cheek look at the week's news.
